will you go. Quan said, to the donor household below the mountain, to become a water buffalo
there. He said, may I follow the master there. Quan said, if youThe wording differs here and there, but how could the school differ
would follow me, come holding a blade of grass in your mouth. I said, this water buffalo is not the same as
the śramaṇa water buffalo. You must be very careful, only then will you not be confused at the time.

Asked, what is the other-kind of coming and going. I said, not yet knowing one has oneself. Then asked,
all speech, sounds, forms, right and wrong are altogether the other-kind of coming and going. What is
the other-kind within sameness. I said, not choosing its body. What is the fur-wearing and horn-bearingbodhisattva
other-kind. I said, not setting up contact and purity. Also, at another time I answered: on meeting contact, contact;śramaṇashould be
on meeting purity, purity. What is the other-kind in the school. I said, if you want your head,
then grind it off and take it away.

A monk brought this up. Because a monk asked Great Master Dongshan about śramaṇa practice, the Master said, head
three feet long, neck two inches short. What is this meaning. I said, a surpassingThe new and old editions have no difference
phrase, a wondrous phrase. What is called a surpassing phrase, a wondrous phrase. I said, surpassing and wondrous phrases
have three kinds. First, the World-Honored One appeared in the world and for forty-nine years set forth expedient means.
The twelve divisions of teaching, the hundred thousand samadhi wondrous gates, penetrating each gate to the end, all are
surpassing phrases and wondrous phrases. This is speech from the side of transcendence. Second, going from ordinary to holy, thoroughly
penetrating oneself and being no different from Buddha, attaining no remainder or leakage, only then attaining the whole body, only then called
one mote, one thought, the ten-direction Bhagavans, one road, the gate of nirvana. When one reaches
such a time, one does not abide in the proper position and does not choose one’s body, but instead enters the other kind, wearing
fur and bearing horns without a thought of difference. Therefore it is said, no class of things can compare with it.
All Buddhas and patriarchs cannot reckon or measure it. Therefore the ancients said, in śramaṇa speech one must not
use measurements and dimensions to speak to people. Therefore it is called a surpassing phrase, a wondrous phrase. This is on the side of form-kind
speech. Third, when nothing whatsoever can be compared to it, only then is it called a surpassing
phrase, a wondrous phrase. Therefore the ancients said, a thousand kinds cannot compare, and the ten thousand things cannot illustrate it.
The wise cannot know it; those of highest capacity also do not recognize it. It is also said to be originally without
likeness. Therefore it is a surpassing phrase, a wondrous phrase. A surpassing phrase, a wondrous phrase, is that which heaven and humanity cannot measure.
It is a matter beyond measurement. Therefore the ancients called it the phrase surpassing beginning and end. Borrowing this
as speech from the side of language-kind, Xingchou the patched-robe monk asked, what is form-kind.Further below it is more detailed
I said, wearing fur and bearing horns. What is language-kind. He said, Caoshan has only one
pair of eyebrows. What is the water buffalo. He said, dim and muddled-eyed. What is the meaning of this.
He said, not knowing there is heaven and earth.

Chou further cited: a senior monk asked Yunju, the ancient Buddha Dongshan said, from youth
I raised one son. His head is three feet long and his neck two inches short. Up to
now I can do nothing about him. What is that son raised from youth. Ju said,
daily provision, hard to forget. What is head three feet long. He said, no way to manage it. What
is neck two inches short. He said, up to now can you still do anything about it or not. Asked, what is daily provision hard to forget. I said, it is just always present. WhatOne edition has later it raises a question to the master. One
One edition connects it above and still treats it as Yunju's words
is always present. He said, not turning away from it is it. What is no way to manage it. He said,
when it reaches such a time, what person could do anything about it. Up to now, can you still do anything about it or not.
What is the meaning of this. He said, the Buddhas of the three times cannot do anything about it. What
One edition ends Yunju Mountain's words here
is head three feet long, neck two inches short. He said, it is not a matter from the very beginning. What
is the matter from the very beginning. He said, what would you call it.

Asked, what practice does the śramaṇa practice. He said, the practice of beasts. What is the beast
practice. He said, wearing fur and bearing horns. What is śramaṇa practice. He said, in each and every thingFur-clad and horned
without interruption. What is the uninterrupted matter. He said, only then is it practice. What is
the person who wears fur and bears horns. He said, not fearing karma. Why does one arrive at such a place.
He said, if one does not fear karma, what place could one fail to reach.

Asked, I do not ask about going from ordinary to holy. What about going from holy into ordinary. He said, a water
buffalo. What is the water buffalo. He said, dim and muddled-eyed. What is the meaning of this.
He said, it only thinks of water and grass and knows nothing else. What side-matter does that accomplish. He said,
it is simply meeting grass and eating grass, meeting water and drinking water. The Master again said, this saying has
force. If you wish to know its force, this person does not cling to matters on the śramaṇa side, nor does he enter the various
holy reward positions. This is precisely entering the other kind. This other kind is wearing fur and bearing horns, called
śramaṇa practice. It is also called the baggage-place of śramaṇa practice. It is also called head
three feet long, neck two inches short. If you wish to know this meaning, when one comes to śramaṇa practice one does not wish
to use measurements to divide near and far. One must not speak of Zhang Three or Li Four. And head three
feet long means one only reaches it by going from small to great, and today the work is complete. When one reaches such a time,
it is called a surpassing phrase, a wondrous phrase. Neck two inches short means not sitting in the śramaṇa position,
and also not abiding in the various holy reward positions. Therefore it is neck two inches short. At such a time one must not
speak in terms of fitting or not fitting. Therefore it is said, do not bring measurements here to
calculate and think. Even so, this is still a matter on the side of kind. You must know there is a matter within the other
kind. Did you not see it said: where knowledge does not reach, one must not speak of it. If spoken of, then
horns sprout. Calling it suchness is already change. One must proceed within the other kind.
This is called the empty one position. It is called face-to-face carrying both. It is wholly without fixed marks.
Asked, what is kind. He said, wearing fur and bearing horns. What is the different. He said, what to do,
what to do. What is practice. He said, if you want your head, then chop it off and take it away. As for the other kind,
what side-matter does it accomplish. He said, this matter has two kinds of other-kind. One is
śramaṇa other-kind. The second is situational other-kind. Situational other-kind is the civet-cat
and white ox, that sort of thing. As for śramaṇa other-kind, one gains freedom wherever one touches, and only then does one become unchanging.
It is not like that other one. My former teacher asked me, where are you going. I said, to the unchanging
place. He further said, this unchanging matter has two kinds. First, every
person has the fundamental matter. Second, a person who knows this does not abandon any sounds,
forms, rights and wrongs, and is not stuck on any thing whatsoever. This is called unchanging in every place.
It is also called wearing fur and bearing horns. It is also called entering mud and entering water.Probably a difference in the departing-tone pronunciation
It is also called a fellow of the traveling gear. Asked, what is entering mud and entering water. He said, not
changing. Does he turn the body or not. He said, he does not turn the body. What are this person’s household matters.
He said, the various sages cannot measure them. Why can they not measure them. He said, because he is not the same as
the various sages. This is still a matter on the side of kind. Is there still a higher matter. He said, yes. What
is the higher matter. He said, if I tell you, I fear it will fall onto the side of kind.

cb:div.head: Third, Lamp-LightingThe upright three before and after
cb:div.p:
The Master said, as for before Lamp-Lighting, there are two kinds. First, not yet knowing there is, the same as the blood of kind.
milk. Second is knowing that there is, like obtaining the original thing before intention has even sprouted. This is called the Lamp-Before.
Before the Lamp. One kind of knowing that there is, in comings and goings, speech, sounds, forms, and right and wrong, also does not belong
to the functioning of correct illumination, and also does not receive prediction. It is like blood in the same class as milk. This is a matter on the side of leakage and loss.
This is called the Lamp-After. It is simply that the affairs of the three times are exhausted, inner and outer sentiments are forgotten, and one attains no
interruption. Only then does it first deserve the name of the true Lamp-Lighting, and thus it is said to receive prediction.

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Recorded Sayings of Chan Master Caoshan Yuanzheng of FuzhouEnd

cb:div.head: Collation notes
cb:div.p:
Therefore it says, zi闍T47n1987Bp0538a25X66n1297_p0659c10闇好愛余餘閒間觀官歸來聖勝他也
Carefully, carefully, straight through to老又余西東東西謂者父又我行持誓願謂者勝聖謂者曹山余類麼否和尚余師研斫究穴語邊語本來originally問如thusasks thusChou again cited先師Dongshan, the Ancient Buddhaformer teacher
Now there is nothing to be doneattainstraight on until
up to now, there is nothing to be donefrom youthposition師曰
